questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Who among the following are the only Indian citizens to have won the Nobel Peace Prize as of 2025?
questions.explanation: According to the source material, the Indian citizens who won the Nobel Peace Prize are Mother Teresa (1979) and Kailash Satyarthi (2014). Other names like Amartya Sen or C.V. Raman won in different categories.
questions.takeaway: Memorize the specific Indian laureates for the Peace category.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Indian citizens who won Nobel peace prize: Mother Teresa (1979), Kailash Satyarthi (2014).

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Regarding the regulations governing the Nobel Peace Prize, which of the following statements accurately describes the 'Finality and Transferability' of the award?

options.text: The Nobel Committee reserves the right to revoke the prize if the laureate's subsequent actions contradict the spirit of peace.
The prize money and the official title can be transferred to a designated charitable foundation upon the laureate's request.
Once the prize is announced, the decision is final and the title cannot be shared or revoked, though the physical medal may be gifted.
The physical medal must remain in the permanent possession of the laureate or their legal heirs to maintain the validity of the title.

questions.explanation: According to the prize regulations, the decision is final and cannot be revoked, shared, or transferred once announced. However, there is a distinction between the honor (the title) and the physical object (the medal). The medal can be gifted, loaned, or even auctioned (as seen with Dmitry Muratov), while the title remains non-transferable.
questions.takeaway: The Nobel Peace Prize title is irrevocable and non-transferable, but the physical medal is the laureate's property to gift or auction.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Finality of the Prize: Once announced, it cannot be revoked, shared, or transferred with the decision being final and standing for all time. Transferability of the Medal: While the honour and title are non-transferable, the medal can be gifted, loaned or displayed by someone else.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: The 2025 Nobel Peace Prize was awarded to Maria Corina Machado primarily for her work in which of the following areas?

options.text: Negotiating a nuclear disarmament treaty in Eastern Europe.
Leading humanitarian aid missions for refugees of the Ukrainian War.
Advocating for a just and peaceful transition from dictatorship to democracy in Venezuela.
Developing grassroots movements for climate resilience in South America.

questions.explanation: Maria Corina Machado, a Venezuelan opposition leader, was awarded the 2025 Nobel Peace Prize for her struggle to achieve a just and peaceful transition from dictatorship to democracy in Venezuela.
questions.takeaway: The 2025 Nobel Peace Prize recognizes democratic struggle and political transition in Venezuela.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Nobel Peace Prize for 2025 was awarded to Maria Corina Machado for her struggle to achieve a just and peaceful transition from dictatorship to democracy in Venezuela.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following physical and financial specifications correctly describes the Nobel Peace Prize as of 2025?

options.text: A 24-carat gold medal weighing 200 grams and a cheque of 10 million Swedish crowns.
An 18-carat gold medal weighing 196 grams and a cheque of 11 million Swedish crowns.
A 22-carat gold medal with a diameter of 7.5 centimetres and a cheque of 11 million Swedish crowns.
An 18-carat gold medal with a diameter of 6.6 centimetres and a cheque of 15 million Swedish crowns.

questions.explanation: As per the 2025 data, the Nobel Peace Prize includes a medal of 18 carats gold, weighing 196 grams with a diameter of 6.6 centimetres. The financial component (cheque) is currently 11 million Swedish crowns.
questions.takeaway: The Nobel Prize consists of a specific gold medal (18k, 196g) and a significant financial grant (11m SEK).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Inclusions of the Prize: Medal, a diploma, and a cheque (currently 11 million Swedish crowns). The medal is currently, 18 carats gold, 196 grams with diameter of 6.6 centimetres.
